market_context:
Cohort size: 357 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £175.00
Median 90-day occupancy: 28.3%

analysis.rewritten_desc: As you step into this charming cottage, the warmth and comfort envelop you, making you feel right at home. Located in the heart of the Peak District, this entire home is perfect for 4 guests looking to escape the hustle and bustle. With a rating of 4.87 from 39 reviews, you can trust that you're in good hands. The cottage is nestled in a beautiful location, waiting to be explored. Book now and experience the tranquility of the Peak District for yourself, with 46 open nights available in the next 90 days.
top_amenity_gaps: Outdoor amenities like a garden or patio, which typically affects 25% of bookings
Parking facilities, which typically affects 15% of bookings
Entertainment options like a TV or games, which typically affects 10% of bookings
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a photo of the living room as the cover, showcasing the cosy atmosphere and quality of the cottage, which will immediately draw potential guests in.
analysis.photo_story_arc: The current photo sequence does not tell a story, it is unclear what the narrative order is. A better approach would be to start with an exterior shot, followed by the living room, then the kitchen, and finally the bedrooms and bathrooms, ending with a standout detail like a beautiful view.
key_diagnosis: Your rate (£143) is £32 below the city median (£175) while your occupancy sits at 48.9% — you may be leaving money on the table. Raising by £10/night could likely increase revenue without significantly impacting occupancy.
With 46 open nights in the next 90 days, focusing on filling these nights could increase revenue by £6,598 (46 nights × £143/night).
Your 4.87 rating from 39 reviews is excellent, but a more compelling title and description could increase bookings by emphasizing the unique selling points of the cottage.
analysis.invisible_leverage: The fact that the host's occupancy is nearly double the median occupancy of the cohort (48.9% vs 28.3%) suggests that the host may be able to increase rates without sacrificing bookings, as their property is already outperforming the market.
analysis.pricing_verdict: Raise by £10 — the host's rate is below the median, and their high occupancy suggests they may be able to increase rates without impacting demand.
analysis.revenue_diagnosis: The host is losing £1,029 per month due to not having a strong title and description, as this is affecting their visibility and appeal to potential guests. Focusing on improving the title and description could recover this revenue, with 46 open nights × £143/night = £6,598 potential revenue, and £1,029 being a conservative estimate of the potential increase.
analysis.outreach_hook: With 48.9% occupancy in the next 90 days, you're outpacing the local market, but your £143 rate is £32 below the median, leaving potential revenue on the table.
